data.title: ReelTime Slashes Potential Dilution by Over 74% with Historic Capital Reset
data.description: While companies like NVIDIA, Alphabet, Palantir, Microsoft, and Meta Platforms are increasing their share counts to raise funds for AI investments, ReelTime Media is taking a different approach. Instead of diluting their shares, ReelTime Media is focusing on strengthening per-share value. This strategy sets them apart in the market and may attract investors looking for companies with a more conservative approach to capital-intensive projects.
Bond Tap Issue placed successfully with $500 million raised.

data.title: ReelTime Media Cuts Outstanding Debt in Half, Bringing Total Debt Reduction Over the Past Year Down More Than 64%
data.description: As tech giants Nvidia, Oracle, AMD, Alphabet, and Microsoft are increasing their borrowing to invest in costly AI infrastructure, ReelTime is focusing on reducing debts and improving its financial position. The companies are seeking funds for AI projects, with Nvidia alone borrowing $5 billion, Oracle seeking $20 billion, AMD looking for $2.5 billion, Alphabet borrowing $10 billion, and Microsoft planning to borrow $30 billion. On the other hand, ReelTime is concentrating on managing its debt and transforming its balance sheet, adopting a disciplined approach towards financial stability amidst the technological advancements in the industry.

data.title: Elme Communities Provides Update On Liquidation Activities
data.description: A company has finalized purchase and sale agreements for three out of the 10 properties left, with the closing anticipated in the first quarter of 2026. The company aims to conclude all remaining sales by mid-year 2026. The total estimated liquidating distributions have been adjusted to fall within the range of $17.02 to $17.47 per share. This includes the initial distribution of $14.67 per share that was paid out in January 2026.

data.title: Bank, Private-Equity Stock Rout Deepens As AI Fears Mount: What's Moving Markets Friday?
data.description: On Friday, a significant banking selloff rocked Wall Street amidst concerns of economic disruption driven by artificial intelligence. This week saw a heightened unease, exacerbated by the release of hotter-than-expected producer price data. This clouded the Federal Reserve's anticipated rate-cut trajectory. By 1:00 p.m. in New York, major indices showed declines: the Nasdaq 100 dropped by 0.6%, the S&P 500 fell by 0.7%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average tumbled by 1.3%, and the Russell 2000 declined by 2.1%. The financial sector bore the brunt of the downturn, with prominent institutions such as Goldman Sachs Group Inc., Wells Fargo & Co., Bank of America Corp., Morgan Stanley and Citigroup Inc. experiencing significant drops ranging from 5% to 7%. This marked their worst performances since early April. Investor anxiety also extended to diversified financials, with Jefferies Financial Group Inc. and Western Alliance Bancorp sinking by 11%. Moreover, private equity firms like Blackstone Inc., Blue Owl Capital Inc., and Ares experienced broad declines, raising concerns about impairments. Blackstone Inc. fell by 4.58%, Blue Owl Capital Inc. by 6.06%, while more details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: VERSES® Restructures to Focus on Core Target Market
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on January 16, 2026, VERSES AI Inc. (CBOE: VERS) (OTCQB: VRSSF), known as "VERSES," a cognitive computing company, shared a new corporate strategy. This strategy aims to focus resources on the company's main target markets and top initiatives.

data.title: PainReform Ltd. Changes Name to PRF Technologies Ltd. Reflecting Expansion into Diversified Healthcare and AI-Driven Energy Platforms
data.description: At the annual general meeting on January 6, 2026, shareholders overwhelmingly approved a name change for the company, signaling a shift away from a single-asset focus. The decision reflects the company's strategic evolution, with a significant majority of shareholders voting in favor of the change. The new name signifies a broader vision and growth trajectory for the company, aligning with its expansion into diverse sectors. This pivotal move highlights the company's commitment to adapt to market dynamics and capitalize on emerging opportunities, setting the stage for enhanced shareholder value and long-term sustainability.

data.title: Federal Reserve Privately Moves To Quash DOJ Subpoenas In Jerome Powell Investigation: Report
data.description: The Federal Reserve is pushing back against two subpoenas issued as part of a criminal investigation into Chair Jerome Powell by U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro. The Fed is reportedly seeking to have the subpoenas dismissed in sealed proceedings to potentially reduce or eliminate its obligation to respond. The specific legal grounds being asserted in the case have not been disclosed, but it is common for parties in high-profile matters to challenge subpoenas as overly broad or seeking privileged information. This legal battle is being conducted behind closed doors due to secrecy rules surrounding criminal investigations before a grand jury. The Federal Reserve has not provided immediate comment in response to Benzinga's request. This challenge arises amidst escalating tensions between the Federal Reserve and the Trump administration. data.title: Nevis development project by crypto millionaire offers residents $100/month if approved, facing local backlash: FT.
data.description: Olivier Janssens has proposed a project called Destiny, offering Nevis residents $100 monthly if the government greenlights the development. The initiative has sparked backlash from locals, as per the Financial Times.

data.title: Premier Fence partners with SecurityWorks in a strategic move.
data.description: A recent report shows that unemployment rates in the US have reached 5.2%, rising slightly from the previous month. This uptick represents approximately 8.4 million Americans who are currently jobless. Additionally, the number of new job openings decreased by 105,000, totaling 10.4 million available jobs. The sectors most affected by these changes include leisure and hospitality, professional and business services, and retail trade. Experts predict a slow but steady recovery in the labor market in the coming months.

data.title: Childhelp Launches "15 Seconds to Safety" Campaign with Support from Claire Holt, Dolph Lundgren, Mena Suvari, and Other Advocates
data.description: In Scottsdale, Arizona on March 13, 2026, it was reported that a child abuse report is made every 15 seconds in the United States. This alarming statistic emphasizes the critical need for immediate action to address child abuse. Childhelp, a national nonprofit organization focusing on the prevention and treatment of child abuse, is at the forefront of raising awareness and combating this issue.

data.title: S&P 500 Ulta Beauty Earnings Are Ugly But This Is Why Analysts Remain Positive
data.description: On Friday, Ulta Beauty saw a decline in its shares despite analysts holding a positive outlook on the cosmetics company. Despite the unimpressive earnings reported for S&P 500 Ulta Beauty, experts remain bullish on the company's future prospects.

data.title: Boeing Contracts Get $2.49 Bil. Update; KC-135s Collide Over Iraq
data.description: During Operation Epic Fury, two KC-135 aircraft were involved in an in-flight incident over Iraq. Following this, Boeing, Lockheed Martin, and RTX were awarded substantial contracts. Boeing contracts received a total update of $2.49 billion.

data.title: Job Openings Bounce, Inflation Hot, Keeping Fed On Hold; S&P 500 Rises (Live Coverage)
data.description: Inflation has been a continuing issue prior to the Iran conflict. Following a surge in job openings, inflation remains high, prompting the Federal Reserve to maintain its current stance. Concurrently, the S&P 500 saw an increase in value.

data.title: Children's Hospital of Philadelphia's Susan C. Nicolson, MD, Honored with Congenital Cardiac Anesthesia Society Lifetime Achievement Award
data.description: Today, Children's Hospital of Philadelphia (CHOP) disclosed that Dr. Susan C. Nicolson, an attending anesthesiologist, has significantly influenced the training and implementation of congenital cardiac anesthesia globally. Dr. Nicolson's groundbreaking efforts have had a profound impact on this field.
